title: A calm update when the timeline may slip
summary: A short project update that explains a delay without sounding defensive.
practiceFocus: Softening bad news and proposing a timeline adjustment.

sentences.english: I wanted to give a quick update on where things stand.
sentences.chinese: 我想快速说明一下目前的进展。
sentences.note: "Where things stand" means the current situation.

sentences.english: Most of the work is moving in the right direction, but one part may need a bit more time.
sentences.chinese: 大部分工作方向是对的，但其中一部分可能需要多一点时间。
sentences.note: This softens the problem by placing it inside wider progress.

sentences.english: I do not want to overstate the issue, but I think it is worth flagging early.
sentences.chinese: 我不想把问题说得太严重，但我觉得值得早点提出来。
sentences.note: "Flagging early" sounds proactive in NZ/AU workplace English.

sentences.english: If we adjust the timeline now, we can avoid rushing the final review later.
sentences.chinese: 如果我们现在调整时间线，就可以避免后面最终审核太赶。
sentences.note: This frames the delay as risk management, not an excuse.

expressions.meaning: important enough to mention before it grows
prevent the review from becoming hurried

shadowingLine: I do not want to overstate the issue, but I think it is worth flagging early.

title: Clarifying who owns the next step
summary: A simple meeting exchange that checks ownership without sounding pushy.
practiceFocus: Asking for ownership, timing, and next steps.

sentences.english: Before we move on, can I quickly check who is taking this forward?
sentences.chinese: 在我们继续之前，我能快速确认一下谁来推进这件事吗？
sentences.note: "Taking this forward" means owning the next step.

sentences.english: I am happy to draft the first version if that helps.
sentences.chinese: 如果有帮助的话，我可以先起草第一版。
sentences.note: A practical way to offer help without overcommitting.

sentences.english: Would Friday be a realistic time to share it back with the group?
sentences.chinese: 周五把它再发回给大家，这个时间现实吗？
sentences.note: "Realistic" is softer than asking if something is possible.

expressions.meaning: a soft way to offer support
a time that is practical and achievable

shadowingLine: Can I quickly check who is taking this forward?

title: Pushing back when the scope grows
summary: A careful response when extra work is being added late.
practiceFocus: Pushing back with reasons while keeping a collaborative tone.

sentences.english: I understand why we would want to include that, but it may change the scope more than it first appears.
sentences.chinese: 我理解为什么我们想把它包括进去，但它对范围的影响可能比一开始看起来更大。
sentences.note: Start with understanding before raising risk.

sentences.english: My concern is that we would be adding a new workstream quite late in the process.
sentences.chinese: 我担心的是，我们会在流程比较后期增加一条新的工作线。
sentences.note: "My concern is" is clear but not aggressive.

sentences.english: Could we capture it as a follow-up item rather than folding it into this release?
sentences.chinese: 我们能不能把它记录成后续事项，而不是并入这次发布？
sentences.note: Offers an alternative instead of simply saying no.

expressions.meaning: increase or alter what the project includes
including it in the current release

shadowingLine: It may change the scope more than it first appears.

title: Summarising a decision at the end of a meeting
summary: A meeting close that confirms the decision, owner, and next checkpoint.

sentences.english: Just to summarise, we have agreed to keep the current approach for now.
sentences.chinese: 简单总结一下，我们同意目前先保持现有方法。
sentences.note: "For now" leaves room for later changes.

sentences.english: Mia will check the numbers, and I will update the notes by tomorrow morning.
sentences.chinese: Mia 会核对数字，我会在明早前更新记录。
sentences.note: Clear owner plus deadline.

sentences.english: If anything changes, we will pick it up again in Thursday's check-in.
sentences.chinese: 如果有变化，我们会在周四的同步会上再讨论。
sentences.note: "Pick it up again" means return to the topic.

expressions.meaning: a phrase for closing and confirming

shadowingLine: Just to summarise, we have agreed to keep the current approach for now.

title: Answering a strength question without sounding rehearsed
summary: A concise interview response that turns a personal strength into a workplace example.

sentences.english: One strength I would point to is that I tend to bring structure to unclear situations.
sentences.chinese: 我会提到的一个优势是，我通常能给不清晰的情况建立结构。
sentences.note: "I would point to" sounds less stiff than "my strength is".

sentences.english: For example, in my last role, I often helped turn broad requests into clear next steps.
sentences.chinese: 比如在上一份工作中，我经常帮助把宽泛的需求转成清晰的下一步。
sentences.note: "Broad requests" is a useful workplace chunk for vague tasks.

sentences.english: I find that people work better when the goal, owner, and timeline are easy to see.
sentences.chinese: 我发现当目标、负责人和时间线清晰时，大家会协作得更好。
sentences.note: This sentence shows judgement, not just a trait.

expressions.chinese: 给不清晰的情况建立结构

expressions.text: turn broad requests into clear next steps
expressions.chinese: 把宽泛需求转成清晰下一步

expressions.meaning: three practical pieces that make work accountable

shadowingLine: I tend to bring structure to unclear situations.

title: Why this role feels like the right next step
summary: A focused answer for explaining motivation without sounding generic.
practiceFocus: Connecting personal direction with company needs.

sentences.english: What interests me about this role is the mix of problem solving and stakeholder communication.
sentences.chinese: 这个职位吸引我的地方是它结合了解决问题和利益相关方沟通。
sentences.note: "What interests me" is direct and natural.

sentences.english: I am looking for a role where I can be close to the work, but also help make decisions clearer for others.
sentences.chinese: 我在找一个既能接近实际工作，又能帮助别人把决策变清晰的职位。
sentences.note: This explains what kind of work environment you want.

sentences.english: From what I have read, that seems to be a big part of how this team operates.
sentences.chinese: 从我了解到的信息看，这似乎正是这个团队运作方式中的重要部分。
sentences.note: Shows research without overclaiming.

expressions.text: the mix of problem solving and stakeholder communication
expressions.meaning: two parts of the role that appeal to you
expressions.chinese: 解决问题和利益相关方沟通的结合

shadowingLine: I am looking for a role where I can be close to the work.

title: Talking about a mistake maturely
summary: A behavioural answer that owns a mistake and explains what changed afterwards.

sentences.english: Earlier in my career, I underestimated how much context a stakeholder needed before approving a change.
sentences.chinese: 在职业早期，我低估了利益相关方在批准变更前需要多少背景信息。
sentences.note: "Earlier in my career" creates distance without avoiding responsibility.

sentences.english: The decision was delayed because I had answered the technical question but not the business concern behind it.
sentences.chinese: 决策被延迟了，因为我回答了技术问题，却没有回应其背后的业务顾虑。
sentences.note: A mature explanation distinguishes surface issue and real concern.

sentences.english: Since then, I have made a point of checking what decision people are trying to make, not just what question they are asking.
sentences.chinese: 从那以后，我会特意确认大家想做什么决策，而不仅仅是他们在问什么问题。
sentences.note: "Made a point of" means deliberately made it a habit.

expressions.meaning: did not realise enough background was needed
expressions.chinese: 低估了需要多少背景信息

expressions.meaning: deliberately made a habit of checking

shadowingLine: I answered the technical question but not the business concern behind it.

title: Discussing salary expectations carefully
summary: A polite way to discuss salary range without sounding fixed too early.

sentences.english: I am still learning more about the scope of the role, so I would prefer to think in terms of a range.
sentences.chinese: 我还在进一步了解这个职位的范围，所以我更倾向于以范围来考虑。
sentences.note: This avoids locking into a number too early.

sentences.english: Based on the market and my experience, I would be looking somewhere in the mid to high range for this level.
sentences.chinese: 基于市场情况和我的经验，这个级别我会考虑中高区间。
sentences.note: "Somewhere in" leaves room to discuss.

sentences.english: I am open to discussing the full package once we know there is a strong fit.
sentences.chinese: 如果双方匹配度高，我也愿意讨论整体待遇包。
sentences.note: "Full package" includes salary, benefits, leave, flexibility.

expressions.meaning: thinking with a salary range, not one number

expressions.text: somewhere in the mid to high range
expressions.meaning: around the middle-to-upper part of the range

shadowingLine: I would prefer to think in terms of a range.

title: Moving from context to recommendation
summary: A presentation transition that connects background information to a recommendation.

sentences.english: So that gives us the background, but the main question is what we do next.
sentences.chinese: 这就是背景情况，但主要问题是接下来我们该怎么做。
sentences.note: This helps listeners move from context to action.

sentences.english: Based on what we have seen so far, I would recommend a staged rollout.
sentences.chinese: 基于目前看到的情况，我建议分阶段推出。
sentences.note: "Based on what we have seen so far" keeps the recommendation grounded.

sentences.english: That would let us test demand while keeping the operational risk manageable.
sentences.chinese: 这样可以让我们测试需求，同时把运营风险控制在可管理范围内。
sentences.note: Explains the business reason behind the recommendation.

expressions.chinese: 把运营风险控制在可管理范围内

shadowingLine: I would recommend a staged rollout.

title: Handling a question you cannot fully answer yet
summary: A calm response when you need to follow up after a presentation question.

sentences.english: That is a good question, and I do not want to give you a rough answer without checking the detail.
sentences.chinese: 这是个好问题，我不想在没有核对细节的情况下给你一个粗略答案。
sentences.note: This avoids guessing and protects credibility.

sentences.english: What I can say at this point is that the trend is moving in the right direction.
sentences.chinese: 我现在可以说的是，趋势正在朝正确方向发展。
sentences.note: "At this point" signals a partial answer.

sentences.english: I will confirm the exact number and come back to you after the session.
sentences.chinese: 我会确认准确数字，并在会后回复你。
sentences.note: A clear follow-up promise.

expressions.meaning: an approximate answer that may not be accurate

expressions.chinese: 目前 / 现在这个阶段

shadowingLine: I do not want to give you a rough answer without checking the detail.

title: Explaining what a chart actually means
summary: A simple chart explanation that avoids reading every number.

sentences.english: The main thing to notice here is the steady increase from March onwards.
sentences.chinese: 这里最主要要注意的是，从三月开始的稳定增长。
sentences.note: "The main thing to notice" guides the listener.

sentences.english: There are a few small dips, but the overall direction is still positive.
sentences.chinese: 中间有几次小幅下降，但整体方向仍然是积极的。
sentences.note: "Dips" means small decreases.

sentences.english: For us, that suggests the change is starting to have an effect.
sentences.chinese: 对我们来说，这说明这个变化开始产生效果。
sentences.note: Move from data to meaning.

expressions.meaning: the key point to pay attention to

shadowingLine: The overall direction is still positive.

title: Closing a presentation with a clear ask
summary: A closing paragraph that turns a recommendation into a decision request.

sentences.english: To wrap up, the recommendation is to proceed with the pilot for one quarter.
sentences.chinese: 最后总结一下，建议是推进一个季度的试点。
sentences.note: "To wrap up" signals the close.

sentences.english: The investment is modest, and the learning value is high enough to justify it.
sentences.chinese: 投入不算大，而学习价值足够高，可以证明它是值得的。
sentences.note: "Justify it" means make it worth doing.

sentences.english: What we need today is agreement on the pilot scope and the success measures.
sentences.chinese: 我们今天需要的是就试点范围和成功衡量标准达成一致。
sentences.note: A clear ask helps the audience decide.

expressions.meaning: make it worth the cost or effort
ways to judge whether something worked

shadowingLine: What we need today is agreement on the pilot scope and the success measures.

title: Monday small talk before work starts
summary: A short office exchange that sounds friendly without oversharing.

sentences.english: How was your weekend? Did you get up to much?
sentences.chinese: 你周末怎么样？有做什么特别的事吗？
sentences.note: "Get up to much" is common in NZ/AU casual English.

sentences.english: Nothing too exciting, just caught up on a few things at home.
sentences.chinese: 没什么特别的，就是在家处理了一些事情。
sentences.note: A natural low-key answer when you do not want to share details.

sentences.english: That sounds like the kind of weekend you sometimes need.
sentences.chinese: 听起来就是那种有时候很需要的周末。
sentences.note: A warm response that keeps the exchange light.

expressions.text: caught up on a few things
the kind of weekend you sometimes need
expressions.meaning: a quiet weekend that helps you reset
expressions.chinese: 有时候正需要的那种周末

shadowingLine: Nothing too exciting, just caught up on a few things at home.

title: Asking about a rental inspection
summary: A polite message to confirm a property viewing time.

sentences.english: Hi, I am calling about the viewing for the flat on King Street.
sentences.chinese: 你好，我打电话是想问 King Street 那套公寓的看房。
sentences.note: "Flat" is common in NZ English.

sentences.english: I just wanted to check whether the Saturday slot is still available.
sentences.chinese: 我想确认一下周六那个时间段是否还可以预约。
sentences.note: "Slot" means an available appointment time.

sentences.english: If not, I would be happy to come by early next week.
sentences.chinese: 如果不行的话，我也可以下周初过去。
sentences.note: "Come by" means visit briefly.

expressions.meaning: visit near the start of next week

shadowingLine: I just wanted to check whether the Saturday slot is still available.

title: Explaining a symptom at a clinic
summary: A clear way to explain symptoms without overcomplicating the story.

sentences.english: I have had this cough for about a week, and it seems to be worse at night.
sentences.chinese: 我咳嗽大概一周了，而且晚上似乎更严重。
sentences.note: "Seems to be" is useful when you are not completely certain.

sentences.english: I do not feel too unwell otherwise, but I wanted to get it checked.
sentences.chinese: 除此之外我没有特别不舒服，但我想检查一下。
sentences.note: "Get it checked" is natural for seeing a doctor.

sentences.english: I have not taken anything apart from some cough syrup.
sentences.chinese: 除了止咳糖浆，我没有吃其他药。
sentences.note: "Apart from" means except for.

expressions.meaning: have a professional look at it

shadowingLine: I wanted to get it checked.

title: Emailing a school about an absence
summary: A concise message to explain a child's absence.

sentences.english: I am writing to let you know that Emma will be away from school today.
sentences.chinese: 我写信是想告知 Emma 今天不能去学校。
sentences.note: "Let you know" is a natural notification phrase.

sentences.english: She has a temperature and needs to rest at home.
sentences.chinese: 她发烧了，需要在家休息。
sentences.note: "Has a temperature" is common for fever.

sentences.english: Please let me know if there is anything we should catch up on tomorrow.
sentences.chinese: 如果明天有什么需要补上的内容，请告诉我。
sentences.note: "Catch up on" means complete missed work.

shadowingLine: Please let me know if there is anything we should catch up on tomorrow.

title: Disagreeing gently in a team discussion
summary: A soft disagreement that keeps the conversation collaborative.

sentences.english: I can see the logic behind that approach.
sentences.chinese: 我能理解这个方法背后的逻辑。
sentences.note: Acknowledge the other person before disagreeing.

sentences.english: My only hesitation is whether it gives us enough room to test the idea properly.
sentences.chinese: 我唯一有点犹豫的是，它是否给了我们足够空间来充分测试这个想法。
sentences.note: "My only hesitation" is softer than "I disagree".

sentences.english: Would it be worth starting with a smaller pilot before we commit fully?
sentences.chinese: 在完全投入之前，是否值得先做一个小规模试点？
sentences.note: A question form lowers defensiveness.

expressions.text: I can see the logic behind that
expressions.meaning: I understand why this makes sense
a polite way to introduce a concern

shadowingLine: My only hesitation is whether it gives us enough room to test the idea properly.

title: Making a request without sounding demanding
summary: A polite request that gives context and room to respond.

sentences.english: Would you have a bit of time this week to look over the draft?
sentences.chinese: 你这周有没有一点时间帮我看一下草稿？
sentences.note: "Would you have a bit of time" is softer than "Can you".

sentences.english: No pressure if you are tied up; I can also ask someone else to sanity-check it.
sentences.chinese: 如果你很忙也没关系，我也可以请别人帮我快速把关。
sentences.note: "Tied up" means busy.

sentences.english: I mainly want to make sure the tone feels right before it goes out.
sentences.chinese: 我主要是想确保在发出去之前语气是合适的。
sentences.note: Explaining the reason makes the request easier to accept.

shadowingLine: No pressure if you are tied up.

title: Receiving feedback without becoming defensive
summary: A response that shows you understand feedback and will act on it.
practiceFocus: Acknowledging feedback and asking for examples.

sentences.english: Thanks for pointing that out; I can see how the message may have landed that way.
sentences.chinese: 谢谢你指出来；我能理解那条信息为什么可能会给人那样的感觉。
sentences.note: "Landed that way" means was received or interpreted that way.

sentences.english: Could you give me an example of where the tone felt too direct?
sentences.chinese: 你能给我一个例子，哪里语气感觉太直接了吗？
sentences.note: Asking for an example keeps the conversation practical.

sentences.english: I will adjust it so the ask is still clear but less abrupt.
sentences.chinese: 我会调整一下，让请求仍然清楚但不那么突然。
sentences.note: "Abrupt" means too sudden or blunt.

shadowingLine: I can see how the message may have landed that way.

title: Saying no while preserving the relationship
summary: A careful refusal that explains constraints and offers an alternative.

sentences.english: I would like to help, but I do not think I can take this on properly this week.
sentences.chinese: 我愿意帮忙，但我觉得这周我没法好好接下这件事。
sentences.note: "Properly" shows you care about quality.

sentences.english: If it can wait until next Tuesday, I could give it the attention it needs.
sentences.chinese: 如果可以等到下周二，我就能给它足够的关注。
sentences.note: Offer a realistic alternative.

sentences.english: Otherwise, it may be better to ask someone who has more capacity right now.
sentences.chinese: 否则，可能请现在更有余力的人会更好。
sentences.note: "Capacity" means available time and energy.

expressions.meaning: accept and do the task well

expressions.text: give it the attention it needs
expressions.meaning: spend enough careful time on it
has more available time or energy

shadowingLine: I do not think I can take this on properly this week.
